_note: MOCK procedure result in the style of synthetic-ambient-fhir-25. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Colorectal cancer screening — prior incomplete exam (2018, inadequate prep); first-degree family history of CRC (father, ~70s)
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 7/9 (right 2, transverse 2, left 3) — adequate. Low-volume split-dose PEG-based prep with pre-procedure ondansetron; patient tolerated prep without vomiting.
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy. INDICATION: colorectal cancer screening; prior 2018 attempt aborted for inadequate preparation; newly reported first-degree family history of colon cancer (father, early 70s). PREP: This time a low-volume split-dose PEG-based preparation was used with pre-procedure ondansetron given the documented 2018 tolerability issue (nausea/vomiting with high-volume PEG — an intolerance, not a true allergy). Patient tolerated the prep without vomiting. Bowel preparation was adequate overall (BBPS 7/9). SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl), well tolerated. FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum, confirmed by identification of the appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve; the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. Unlike the 2018 aborted exam, a complete diagnostic examination was achieved. A 5 mm sessile polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy (specimen A, retrieved). A 4 mm sessile polyp in the sigmoid colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy (specimen B, retrieved). Scattered non-complicated diverticula were noted in the sigmoid colon without stigmata of bleeding. Findings consistent with the patient's baseline constipation; no mass, ulceration, stricture, or luminal narrowing. No active bleeding. IMPRESSION: Complete screening colonoscopy to cecum with adequate prep. Two diminutive polyps removed (ascending, sigmoid). Sigmoid diverticulosis. Pathology pending. No alarm features and no concerning mucosal lesion identified.
disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Patient counseled that prep was successfully tolerated with the low-volume/antiemetic strategy and that a complete exam was achieved.

followup.recommendation: Review polyp pathology (ascending and sigmoid specimens); call patient with results and set surveillance interval

Set surveillance interval accounting for first-degree family history of CRC (father, ~70s) in addition to polyp pathology; family history was newly reported and should be documented in the problem list
Ensure chart correction persists: PEG bowel prep is an intolerance (nausea/vomiting), not a true allergy — retain low-volume PEG + antiemetic strategy for future preps

planted_followup_items.description: Polypectomy pathology pending for two specimens (ascending colon 5 mm, sigmoid colon 4 mm); results must be communicated to the patient and drive the surveillance interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with results + document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance interval must incorporate newly reported first-degree family history of colorectal cancer (father, early 70s) alongside polyp histology; risk is above average, so a shorter interval than standard low-risk screening may apply
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set and document surveillance interval at pathology review, factoring family history

planted_followup_items.description: Newly reported first-degree family history of CRC (father, ~70s) surfaced pre-procedure and is not in the chart problem list; must be documented to inform future screening
planted_followup_items.expected_action: add family history of colorectal cancer to problem list

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm pretty bloated and passing a lot of gas today.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatulence
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red flags; self-care guidance only.

contacts.verbatim: When I wiped after a bowel movement this morning there was a little pink on the toilet paper. Should I be worried? I feel completely fine otherwise, no pain, no dizziness.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): minor scant spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy in a patient on NO anticoagulant, with no volume of blood, no clots, no pain, and no hemodynamic symptoms — expected minor self-limited oozing, not delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage
contacts.expected_action: Reassure: a small amount of pink/spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited, especially off anticoagulation. Provide return precautions — call back or seek care for repeated passage of a moderate/large amount of red blood or clots, ongoing bleeding, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Scant spotting with no bleeding volume, pain, or hemodynamic symptoms after tiny cold-snare polypectomy off anticoagulation is benign; the trap is that any post-polypectomy 'blood' reads as urgent but this presentation is reassure-with-precautions, not escalate.

contacts.verbatim: It's been over a week — when will I find out what the biopsies showed, and does my dad's colon cancer change how often I need this done?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology results and how the newly reported family history affects her surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: two diminutive polyps removed, pathology expected back ~2026-08-11; the team will call with results and set the surveillance interval, which will also factor in her father's colon cancer (first-degree family history raises risk above average and may shorten the interval).
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item (F1) and the surveillance/family-history items (F2, F3).
